# UC-22.14.6 · Audit Reduction and Report Generation Integrity

## Description

Tracks scheduled searches that materialize audit reports to ensure AU-7 reduction and aggregation does not remove required contextual fields.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes _audit, summary. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Prefix audit-export saved searches with a standard name; (2) validate fields in summary vs raw sample monthly; (3) alert on `sla_breach`; (4) document field retention in the audit reduction procedure. Indexes required: `index=_audit`, `index=summary`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `_audit` (search, savedsearch_name, info), summary indexes `index=summary`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Prefix audit-export saved searches with a standard name; (2) validate fields in summary vs raw sample monthly; (3) alert on `sla_breach`; (4) document field retention in the audit reduction procedure.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=_audit action=search info=completed earliest=-24h
| search savedsearch_name="*AU7*" OR savedsearch_name="*audit_report*"
| stats avg(total_run_time) as avg_runtime, max(total_run_time) as max_runtime, count by savedsearch_name, user
| eval sla_breach=if(avg_runtime>300,1,0)
```

Understanding this SPL

**Audit Reduction and Report Generation Integrity (AU-7)** — Tracks scheduled searches that materialize audit reports to ensure AU-7 reduction and aggregation does not remove required contextual fields.

Documented **Data sources**: `_audit` (search, savedsearch_name, info), summary indexes `index=summary`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: _audit.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=_audit, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by savedsearch_name, user** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **sla_breach** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare a sample of the same events in the source system (domain controller, cloud tenant, or device) the security team would open first. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (saved search performance), Single value (breach count), Time chart (runtime trend)

## SPL

```spl
index=_audit action=search info=completed earliest=-24h
| search savedsearch_name="*AU7*" OR savedsearch_name="*audit_report*"
| stats avg(total_run_time) as avg_runtime, max(total_run_time) as max_runtime, count by savedsearch_name, user
| eval sla_breach=if(avg_runtime>300,1,0)
```

## Visualization

Table (saved search performance), Single value (breach count), Time chart (runtime trend). Key fields: `avg_runtime`, `max_runtime`, `savedsearch_name`, `sla_breach`, `user`.
